title: [CI:DOCS] Configure Renovate
body:
[![Mend Renovate](https://app.renovatebot.com/images/banner.svg)](https://renovatebot.com) Welcome to [Renovate](https://togithub.com/renovatebot/renovate)! This is an onboarding PR to help you understand and configure settings before regular Pull Requests begin. 🚦 To activate Renovate, merge this Pull Request. To disable Renovate, simply close this Pull Request unmerged. --- ### Detected Package Files * `.github/workflows/check_cirrus_cron.yml` (github-actions) * `.github/workflows/stale.yml` (github-actions) * `go.mod` (gomod) ### Configuration 🔡 Renovate has detected a custom config for this PR. Feel free to ask for [help](https://togithub.com/renovatebot/renovate/discussions) if you have any doubts and would like it reviewed. Important: Now that this branch is edited, Renovate can't rebase it from the base branch any more. If you make changes to the base branch that could impact this onboarding PR, please merge them manually. ### What to Expect With your current configuration, Renovate will create 2 Pull Requests:
chore(deps): update actions/stale digest to 0d6f830 - Schedule: ["after 1am and before 11am on Monday"] - Branch name: `renovate/actions-stale-digest` - Merge into: `main` - Upgrade actions/stale to `0d6f83007122d952cf222d03c8a3f6460c3a80fe`
fix(deps): update golang.org/x/term digest to 8365914 - Schedule: ["after 1am and before 11am on the first day of the month"] - Branch name: `renovate/golang.org-x-term-digest` - Merge into: `main` - Upgrade [golang.org/x/term](https://togithub.com/golang/term) to `83659145692c7cf64eba9f213010fa1bc7c502c9`

🚸 Branch creation will be limited to maximum 1 per hour, so it doesn't swamp any CI resources or spam the project. See docs for `prhourlylimit` for details. --- ❓ Got questions? Check out Renovate's [Docs](https://docs.renovatebot.com/), particularly the Getting Started section. If you need any further assistance then you can also [request help here](https://togithub.com/renovatebot/renovate/discussions). --- This PR has been generated by [Mend Renovate](https://www.mend.io/free-developer-tools/renovate/). View repository job log [here](https://app.renovatebot.com/dashboard#github/containers/skopeo).

comments.body:
> update module github.com/containers/common to v1

Uh, that’s a bug. c/common v1.0.0 and v1.0.1 were retracted per the Go semantics, and should not be considered valid updates.

Is this something upstream can fix? (And if not, can we specifically exclude these two versions?)

> Is this something upstream can fix? (And if not, can we specifically exclude these two versions?)

Probably?  Renovate has so many damn buttons...one of these must do the trick...

...hmmm, nothing immediately jumps out at me :disappointed:   I know how to create a custom rule for a single dependency, what I'm afraid of is telling Renovate to (somehow) *ONLY* suggest updates `> v1.0.1` within the golang way of doing things.

@mtrmac do you have a reference for the `retracted per the Go semantics` details?  Maybe if I understand how it was done, and/or what the golang idioms are, I can come at it from that angle.

Actually we only want `0.`_x_ updates. At least for now, c/common has an unstable `0.` API.

Retraction is https://github.com/containers/common/blob/98295c01091c951517c04b50a04211f10181530e/go.mod#L114-L117 , per https://go.dev/ref/mod#go-mod-file-retract .

… so there might be two parts to it:
- Don’t offer 1.0.1
- But do offer 0.51.0 and so on, after these versions become available

I can imagine that the first part could be user-configurable, but the second part makes it trickier. This semantics really should be handled correctly in the primary codebase.

Thanks for the background info.  As near as I can tell, renovate doesn't offer much in terms of an "ignore version X". This maybe a case of adding a config to just blanket-ignore updates to `c/common` - leave it entirely manual.  But I'll keep thinking/looking how best to handle this, perhaps there's a way to do it.

So reading the `go.dev` link, this is the part of the standard that's breaking in Renovate:

    When a user runs go get example.com/m@latest, the go command reads retractions from v1.0.1, which is now the highest 
    version. Both v1.0.0 and v1.0.1 are retracted, so the go command will upgrade (or downgrade!) to the next highest version, 
    perhaps v0.9.5.

This seems like a Renovate bug to me, let me see if I can find any open issues on the topic.
Confirmed, it's a bug in Renovate: https://github.com/renovatebot/renovate/issues/13012

Okay I think I worked around it in a way that's mostly maintainable:  Simple regex list of versions which are unacceptable for this package.  Though that does imply any future retractions will need to be added to the list.
@mtrmac PTAL, I think this is ready to go now.  We can do the same thing as with c/image, and leave dependabot enabled in parallel.  Until you're comfortable turning it off (for dep. updates).
